regimens.notes: Standard regimen for stage I–IV high-grade serous, endometrioid (grade 2/3), clear cell, and carcinosarcoma. For stage I disease: 6 cycles for high-grade serous; 3–6 cycles for all other types. For stage II–IV disease: 6 cycles recommended. Albumin-bound paclitaxel may be substituted for hypersensitivity to paclitaxel. Individuals >70 years or with comorbidities may require alternate dosing.

drugs.name: Bevacizumab
drugs.schedule: Day 1, every 21 days; continue up to 12 additional cycles after chemo

regimens.notes: For stage II–IV disease or previously untreated recurrent disease. Bevacizumab-containing regimens should be used with caution before surgery due to potential interference with postoperative healing. If used as part of neoadjuvant, withhold bevacizumab 4–6 weeks prior to IDS. Bevacizumab continued for up to 12 additional cycles (total including chemo up to 15 months).

drugs.name: Bevacizumab
drugs.schedule: Starting day 1 of cycle 2, every 21 days for up to 22 cycles

regimens.notes: For stage II–IV disease or previously untreated recurrent disease. Median PFS 10.3 months in chemotherapy group vs 14.1 months in bevacizumab-throughout group. No difference in OS between groups.
Similar PFS, OS, and global QOL scores as paclitaxel/carboplatin. May be useful for patients at high risk for neuropathy (eg, diabetes).
Higher response rate but similar PFS and OS as paclitaxel/carboplatin. May be useful for select patients at high risk for neurotoxicity or to avoid alopecia. Repeat every 28 days for 3–6 cycles.
6 cycles (18 weeks). May be considered for those with poor performance status. Albumin-bound paclitaxel may be substituted for hypersensitivity reactions.

For optimally debulked stage II–III disease. Repeat every 21 days for 5–6 cycles. Patients must have normal renal function. Prior to each cycle, adequate IV fluids needed to prevent renal toxicity. Published trial used IV continuous infusion paclitaxel over 24 hours.
Repeat every 21 days for 6–8 cycles. IP carboplatin as part of paclitaxel/carboplatin/bevacizumab combination did not improve PFS or OS compared to IV administration.
Cisplatin associated with higher rates of neurotoxicity, GI toxicities, renal toxicity, metabolic toxicities, anemia, and alopecia compared to carboplatin. Repeat every 21 days for 3–9 cycles.

drugs.name: Bevacizumab
drugs.schedule: Day 1, every 21 days; continue to complete 1 year of therapy

regimens.notes: Repeat every 21 days for 6 cycles, then continue Bevacizumab to complete 1 year of therapy.
For individuals >70 years and/or those with comorbidities. 3–6 cycles. Algorithms have been developed for predicting chemotherapy toxicity.

regimens.regimen_name: Carboplatin weekly/Paclitaxel weekly (alternate dosing for elderly/comorbidities)
regimens.notes: Repeat every 21 days for 6 cycles (18 weeks). For individuals >70 years and/or those with comorbidities.
GI-type chemotherapy for mucinous histology. Retrospective data suggest improved PFS (HR 0.4, 95% CI 0.1–0.97) and OS (HR 0.2, 95% CI 0.1–0.8) with GI regimens compared to gynecologic regimens.
For mucinous carcinoma of the ovary, stage II–IV disease. Alternative GI-type chemotherapy regimen.
For mucinous carcinoma stage II–IV disease. Albumin-bound paclitaxel may be substituted for hypersensitivity.
For mucinous carcinoma stage II–IV disease. Bevacizumab-containing regimen.

regimens.site: Low-Grade Serous Carcinoma / Grade 1 Endometrioid Carcinoma
regimens.notes: For low-grade serous (stage IC)/Grade 1 endometrioid (stage IC) and stage II–IV. Maintenance letrozole is a category 2A recommendation for low-grade serous and category 2B for grade 1 endometrioid. Tamoxifen is NOT recommended for low-grade serous carcinoma.

regimens.site: Low-Grade Serous Carcinoma / Grade 1 Endometrioid Carcinoma
regimens.notes: For low-grade serous (stage IC)/Grade 1 endometrioid (stage IC) and stage II–IV. Aromatase inhibitors are preferred hormonal therapy if not used previously.

regimens.site: Low-Grade Serous Carcinoma / Grade 1 Endometrioid Carcinoma
regimens.notes: For low-grade serous (stage IC)/Grade 1 endometrioid (stage IC) and stage II–IV. Tamoxifen is NOT recommended for low-grade serous carcinoma. Preferred if aromatase inhibitor was given previously.

regimens.site: Low-Grade Serous Carcinoma / Grade 1 Endometrioid Carcinoma

drugs.name: Bevacizumab
drugs.schedule: Day 1, every 21 days; maintenance bevacizumab (ICON-7 & GOG-218)

regimens.notes: For low-grade serous/Grade 1 endometrioid stage II–IV disease. ICON-7 & GOG-218 dosing.

regimens.site: Small Cell Carcinoma of the Ovary (Hypercalcemic Type - SCCOHT)

biomarkers.requirement: Testing recommended if not previously done

regimens.notes: Up to 6 cycles. Based on small case series studies. Consider early referral to stem cell transplant center for autologous stem cell collection at the time of cycle 1.

regimens.site: Small Cell Carcinoma of the Ovary (Hypercalcemic Type - SCCOHT)
regimens.notes: 4–6 cycles. Dose-intensive regimen based on prospective studies in SCCOHT.

regimens.site: Small Cell Carcinoma of the Ovary (Hypercalcemic Type - SCCOHT)
regimens.notes: 3–4 cycles. Repeat every 21 days. Recommend pulmonary function test if considering bleomycin.

regimens.site: Small Cell Carcinoma of the Ovary (Hypercalcemic Type - SCCOHT)
regimens.notes: 4 cycles. Based on small case series studies in SCCOHT.

regimens.site: Small Cell Carcinoma of the Ovary (Hypercalcemic Type - SCCOHT)
regimens.notes: Based on dramatic response to checkpoint inhibitor reported in case report.

regimens.site: Small Cell Carcinoma of the Ovary (Hypercalcemic Type - SCCOHT)
regimens.notes: Dual anti-CTLA-4 and anti-PD-1 blockade. Phase II basket trial of dual anti-CTLA-4 and anti-PD-1 blockade in rare tumors.

regimens.site: Small Cell Carcinoma of the Ovary (Hypercalcemic Type - SCCOHT)
regimens.notes: Based on activity in relapsed epithelial ovarian cancer and absence of a taxane in the primary therapy regimens.

For select patients with stage II–III resected dysgerminoma for whom minimizing toxicity is critical. 3 cycles. Consultation with a pediatric oncologist for pediatric/adolescent patients is recommended.

Repeat every 21 days for 3 cycles for good risk (category 2B), or 4 cycles for poor risk. For any stage embryonal tumors, endodermal sinus tumors, stage II–IV dysgerminoma, stage I grade 2–3 or stage II–IV immature teratoma, and any stage nongestational choriocarcinoma. Recommend pulmonary function test if considering bleomycin.

regimens.regimen_name: TIP (Paclitaxel/Ifosfamide/Mesna/Cisplatin) - Malignant Germ Cell Tumors Recurrence
regimens.notes: Potentially curative regimen for recurrent malignant germ cell tumors. Patients should be referred to tertiary care institution for HCT consultation and potentially curative therapy.

regimens.regimen_name: High-dose chemotherapy + HCT - Malignant Germ Cell Tumors Recurrence
regimens.notes: High-dose chemotherapy regimens vary among institutions. Some patients are potentially curable with HCT. Patients with potentially curable recurrent germ cell disease should be referred to a tertiary care institution for HCT consultation and potentially curative therapy.
Palliative only. For recurrent malignant germ cell tumors.
Palliative only. For recurrent malignant germ cell tumors.
Palliative only. For recurrent malignant germ cell tumors.
Palliative only. For recurrent malignant germ cell tumors.
Palliative only. For recurrent malignant germ cell tumors.
For recurrent malignant germ cell tumors. Useful in certain circumstances.
For recurrent malignant germ cell tumors. Useful in certain circumstances.
For recurrent malignant germ cell tumors. Useful in certain circumstances.

regimens.regimen_name: Carboplatin/Paclitaxel - Sex Cord-Stromal Tumors Primary Therapy
regimens.notes: Preferred regimen for stage II–IV or all others with intermediate risk (eg, heterologous elements). Acceptable options include carboplatin/paclitaxel (preferred), EP, or BEP (category 2B).
Acceptable option for adjuvant therapy of sex cord-stromal tumors (category 2B).
Acceptable option for adjuvant therapy of sex cord-stromal tumors (category 2B). Recommend pulmonary function test if considering bleomycin.
Preferred recurrence therapy for malignant sex cord-stromal tumors.

regimens.regimen_name: Targeted Therapy - Bevacizumab (Sex Cord-Stromal Tumors Recurrence)
regimens.notes: Single agent bevacizumab as targeted therapy for recurrent sex cord-stromal tumors. Useful in certain circumstances.

Combination platinum-based chemotherapy preferred for first recurrence (category 1). OCEANS trial showed improvement in PFS and response rates with addition of bevacizumab. Contraindicated for patients at increased risk of GI perforation. Bevacizumab can be continued as maintenance if response.
Equivalent to carboplatin/paclitaxel but different toxicity profile. May be easier to tolerate.

Combination platinum-based chemotherapy preferred for first recurrence (category 1). GOG-0213 showed bevacizumab slightly increased median OS when combined with chemotherapy (HR 0.829; 95% CI 0.683–1.005; P=.056). Albumin-bound paclitaxel may be substituted for confirmed taxane hypersensitivity.
Platinum-based combination chemotherapy for platinum-sensitive recurrence.
For patients who have been treated with up to three lines of therapy and prior bevacizumab. Steroid premedication should not be used. Phase 3 open-label, randomised, controlled trial.
Preferred cytotoxic therapy for platinum-resistant recurrent ovarian cancer.
Preferred cytotoxic therapy for platinum-resistant recurrent ovarian cancer.
Preferred cytotoxic therapy for platinum-resistant recurrent ovarian cancer.

AURELIA trial showed bevacizumab combined with chemotherapy improved ORR and PFS compared to chemotherapy alone in platinum-resistant disease. Contraindicated for patients at increased risk of GI perforation.
Preferred cytotoxic therapy. Weekly schedule. Albumin-bound paclitaxel may be substituted for hypersensitivity.
Preferred regimen for platinum-resistant recurrent ovarian cancer. AURELIA trial data.
Metronomic oral cyclophosphamide with bevacizumab for platinum-resistant recurrent ovarian cancer.
Preferred cytotoxic therapy for platinum-resistant recurrent ovarian cancer.
Preferred regimen for platinum-resistant recurrent ovarian cancer.

For FRα-expressing tumors (≥75% positive tumor cells). Phase 3 trial showed significantly longer OS compared with chemotherapy (median 16.46 vs 12.75 months; HR for death 0.67; 95% CI 0.50–0.89; P=.005). For patients treated with two prior lines of platinum-based therapy.
For FRα-expressing tumors (≥25% positive tumor cells). Promising activity regardless of FRα expression level or prior bevacizumab treatment.

For HER2-positive tumors (IHC 3+ or 2+). Category 2A for platinum-resistant, category 2B for platinum-sensitive. FDA granted accelerated approval for adult patients with unresectable or metastatic HER2-positive solid tumors. ORR 63.6% in IHC 3+ and 36.8% in IHC 2+ tumors.
For BRAF V600E-positive tumors. NCI-MATCH trial showed ORR 38%, PFS 11.4 months. Clinical benefits observed in 6 patients with primary gynecologic cancer.
NTRK inhibitors for NTRK gene fusion-positive solid tumors. Larotrectinib ORR 79% (95% CI 72%–85%) with 16% CR. FDA approved for NTRK fusion-positive solid tumors.
For RET fusion-positive tumors. Tumor agnostic basket trial showed ORR 43.9% (95% CI 28.5–60.3) in 18 of 41 patients. FDA accelerated approval.

MEK1/2 inhibitor. Randomized trial showed median PFS 13.0 months vs 7.2 months for chemotherapy (HR 0.48; 95% CI 0.36–0.64; P<.0001). ORR 26% vs 6% for chemotherapy. Most common grade 3/4 AEs: skin rash, anemia, hypertension, diarrhea, nausea, fatigue.
Category 2B. Median PFS 9.1 vs 10.6 months for physician's choice chemotherapy (HR 1.21; 95% CI 0.79–1.86; P=.807). Numerically superior ORR (16% vs 13%).

regimens.regimen_name: Avutometinib/Defactinib - KRAS-mutated Low-Grade Serous Carcinoma Recurrence
regimens.notes: For KRAS-mutated tumors (preferred). Phase II trial showed ORR 44% in KRAS-mutant vs 17% in KRAS wild-type. Median PFS 22.0 months vs 12.8 months. Most frequent grade ≥3 AEs: elevated creatine phosphokinase, diarrhea, anemia.
Tested in platinum-sensitive disease treated with ≥2 lines of prior therapy. Found to have consistent efficacy with tolerable safety.
For dMMR/MSI-H recurrent or advanced tumors. ORR 44.1% across multiple tumor types. 3 of 7 ovarian cancer patients had partial response. FDA accelerated approval.

biomarkers.requirement: MSI-H or dMMR, or TMB-H ≥10 mutations/megabase

regimens.notes: For MSI-H/dMMR solid tumors or TMB-H tumors ≥10 mutations/megabase. 15 patients with ovarian cancer included; 3 CR and 2 PR observed. FDA approved for tumor-agnostic indication.

For PD-L1 CPS ≥1 tumors. Tumors with CPS ≥1 are considered PD-L1 positive. Interim analysis showed pembrolizumab significantly improved PFS in PD-L1 CPS ≥1 population (8.3 vs 7.2 months; P=.0014) and OS (18.2 vs 14 months; P=.0053). Phase 3 randomized double-blind trial.

regimens.regimen_name: Ipilimumab + Nivolumab - Platinum-Resistant Clear Cell Carcinoma
regimens.site: Clear Cell Carcinoma of the Ovary
regimens.notes: For clear cell carcinoma. Phase 2 non-randomized trial showed ORR 55%, median duration of response not reached. Grade 3/4 AEs in 35% of patients. Another randomized phase 2 trial showed ORR 26.7% for combination vs 14.2% for nivolumab alone.
GI-type chemotherapy for recurrent mucinous carcinoma. Category 2B for bevacizumab addition.
For low-grade serous carcinoma. Recommended if aromatase inhibitor was given previously.
Category 2B. ORR 18% in phase 2 trial. Grade 3 ALT/AST elevations in 8% of patients.
Platinum agents have limited activity when disease has demonstrated growth through a platinum-based regimen. Platinum rechallenge is generally not recommended in this setting.
Response rate for single-agent bevacizumab is about 20%. May cause hypertension, arterial thrombosis, or intestinal perforation. Contraindicated for patients at increased risk of GI perforation.
Category 2B. Phase 2 superiority trial of niraparib plus bevacizumab versus niraparib alone.

For patients with germline or somatic BRCA1/2 P/LP variants who achieve CR/PR after first-line platinum-based chemotherapy. Up to 2 years unless disease progression or unacceptable toxicity. 7-year follow-up showed 67% alive vs 46% with placebo. Patients who received bevacizumab during primary therapy excluded.

For patients with HRD-positive tumors treated with olaparib/bevacizumab vs bevacizumab monotherapy. Clinically meaningful OS benefit for HRD-positive (HR 0.62; 95% CI 0.45–0.85). Olaparib up to 2 years; bevacizumab up to 15 months total (including chemotherapy). If unable to tolerate olaparib, consider niraparib + bevacizumab.

drugs.name: Niraparib
drugs.schedule: Daily (or 200 mg for patients with baseline body weight <77 kg and/or platelet count <150,000/mm3)

regimens.notes: For BRCA1/2-mutated patients. Up to 3 years unless disease progression or unacceptable toxicity. PRIMA trial showed significant PFS improvement with niraparib vs placebo in HRD (21.9 vs 10.4 months; P<.001) and ITT (13.8 vs 8.2 months). No OS difference observed.
For BRCA1/2-mutated patients. Up to 2 years unless disease progression or unacceptable toxicity. ATHENA-MONO trial demonstrated significant clinical benefit in HRD and overall ITT population.

drugs.name: Niraparib
drugs.schedule: Daily (or 200 mg for patients with baseline body weight <77 kg and/or platelet count <150,000/mm3)

regimens.notes: For patients unable to tolerate olaparib. Niraparib up to 3 years; bevacizumab up to 15 months total. OVARIO phase II trial: 62% of all patients, 76% HRD, 47% HRP, 56% HR unknown remained progression-free at 18 months. 5-year follow-up: median OS 61.1 months overall; not reached in HRD subgroup.

drugs.name: Niraparib
drugs.schedule: Daily (or 200 mg for patients with baseline body weight <77 kg and/or platelet count <150,000/mm3)

regimens.notes: For germline BRCA1/2-mutated platinum-sensitive recurrent ovarian cancer after response to platinum therapy. Until disease progression or unacceptable toxicity. Caution when using maintenance PARPi >24 months. Limited data on prior PARPi use.

For germline or somatic BRCA1/2 P/LP variants with platinum-sensitive recurrent disease after response to platinum therapy. Until disease progression or unacceptable toxicity. Limited data on prior PARPi use.

For germline or somatic BRCA1/2 P/LP variants with platinum-sensitive recurrent disease after response to platinum therapy. Until disease progression or unacceptable toxicity. 21% of rucaparib patients had PFS ≥2 years vs 2% placebo.

biomarkers.requirement: If known, consider maintenance therapy post-primary therapy

regimens.notes: Preferred adjuvant treatment for carcinosarcoma (MMMTs) of the ovary. If known BRCA1/2 mutation, consider maintenance therapy post-primary therapy. If not previously done, consider germline and somatic biomarker testing.
For carcinosarcoma. Useful in certain circumstances.
For carcinosarcoma. Useful in certain circumstances.
Category 2B for carcinosarcoma. Albumin-bound paclitaxel may be substituted for hypersensitivity.

regimens.site: Clear Cell Carcinoma of the Ovary
regimens.notes: For clear cell carcinoma recurrence. Randomized phase III trial compared irinotecan/cisplatin vs paclitaxel/carboplatin as first-line chemotherapy for ovarian clear cell carcinoma.
Single-agent cyclophosphamide for recurrent disease. Other recommended option.
Single-agent doxorubicin for recurrent disease. Other recommended option.
For recurrent platinum-sensitive disease. Other recommended option.
Single-agent paclitaxel for recurrent disease. Other recommended option.

regimens.regimen_name: Albumin-bound Paclitaxel (single agent) - Recurrent Ovarian Cancer
regimens.notes: Single-agent albumin-bound paclitaxel for recurrent disease.
For recurrent platinum-sensitive disease. Other recommended option.
For recurrent platinum-sensitive disease. Other recommended option.
Prolonged oral etoposide as second-line therapy for platinum-resistant and platinum-sensitive ovarian carcinoma.
For recurrent platinum-resistant disease. Other recommended option.
For recurrent platinum-sensitive disease. Other recommended option.
For recurrent platinum-resistant disease. Phase II study showed combination is active.
Category 2B. For those previously treated with taxanes.

regimens.regimen_name: Oral Cyclophosphamide + Pembrolizumab + Bevacizumab - Recurrent Disease
regimens.notes: Phase 2 non-randomized clinical trial showed efficacy and safety of this combination for recurrent ovarian cancer.
Multicentre, randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled, phase 2 trial.

regimens.site: Low-Grade Serous Carcinoma / Sex Cord-Stromal Tumors
regimens.notes: For low-grade serous carcinoma and sex cord-stromal tumors. Aromatase inhibitors preferred if not used previously. Also listed as useful in certain circumstances for sex cord-stromal tumor recurrence.

regimens.regimen_name: Leuprolide acetate or Goserelin acetate - Recurrent Sex Cord-Stromal Tumors
regimens.notes: For granulosa cell tumors. Useful in certain circumstances for sex cord-stromal tumor recurrence.
For sex cord-stromal tumor recurrence. Useful in certain circumstances.

regimens.regimen_name: FOLFOX - Mucinous Carcinoma Recurrence (useful in certain circumstances)
regimens.notes: For recurrent mucinous carcinoma. GI-type chemotherapy regimen.

regimens.regimen_name: CAPOX - Mucinous Carcinoma Recurrence (useful in certain circumstances)
regimens.notes: For recurrent mucinous carcinoma. GI-type chemotherapy regimen.
For age >70 patients with platinum-resistant disease. Useful in certain circumstances.

regimens.regimen_name: Dabrafenib/Trametinib - BRAF V600E-positive Recurrence (Useful in Certain Circumstances)
regimens.notes: Listed under useful in certain circumstances for both platinum-sensitive and platinum-resistant recurrent disease.
Category 2B. For sex cord-stromal tumor recurrence if not previously used.
Category 2B. For sex cord-stromal tumor recurrence if not previously used.
If not previously used. For sex cord-stromal tumor recurrence.
Other recommended option for sex cord-stromal tumor recurrence.

regimens.regimen_name: Paclitaxel (single agent) - Sex Cord-Stromal Tumors Recurrence
regimens.notes: Other recommended option for sex cord-stromal tumor recurrence.
